channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Marriott settles with the FTC for $52 million over data breaches
item.description: Marriott International has agreed to pay $52 million and make changes to bolster its data security to resolve state and federal claims related to major data breaches that affected more than 300 million of its customers worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: TypeScript 5.7 improves error reporting
item.description: TypeScript 5.7, the latest planned release of Microsoft’s JavaScript variant with syntax for types, is now available as a beta release. With the update, the language’s type system is now able to report errors when variables have never been initialized.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Only 'limited by your imagination': Gallium Nitride breakthrough could make LED displays more affordable and convert your smartphone screen into an antenna
item.description: Gallium Nitride breakthrough could make LED displays more affordable and convert your smartphone screen into an antenna
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Rumors Spark Around AMD Radeon RX 7650 GRE Graphics Card: What to Expect in 2025?
item.description: AMD Radeon RX 7650 GRE graphics card rumors surface ahead of CES 2025 event.
item.dc:creator: allisa.james@futurenet.com (Allisa James), TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI sees continued attempts to use AI models for election interference
item.description: OpenAI has seen continued attempts by cybercriminals to use its artificial intelligence (AI) models for fake content aimed at interfering with this year’s elections, the ChatGPT maker said in a new report.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Tesla is unveiling its long-awaited robotaxi amid doubts about the technology it runs on
item.description: Expectations are high for Tesla’s long-awaited robotaxi unveiling at a Hollywood studio Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Tom Krisher, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Here's what has made Hurricane Milton so fierce and unusual
item.description: With its mighty strength and its dangerous path, Hurricane Milton has powered into a very rare threat that is flirting with experts’ worst fears.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Planned Adobe app to put an end to AI training on images and clips
item.description: Adobe has unveiled plans for a new web app called Content Authenticity, which is part of the Content Credentials platform and will be used to ensure that AI models are not trained on content creators’ images, video, or audio clips without their permission.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Creepy yet helpful robot is ready to assist
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: A cyberattack continues to affect the nation's largest regulated water and wastewater utility company, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Shipkowski, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nobel Prize in chemistry honors 3 scientists who used AI to design proteins — life's building blocks
item.description: The Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences has awarded the Nobel Prize in chemistry to David Baker, Demis Hassabis and John Jumper who discovered powerful techniques to predict and even design novel proteins — the building blocks of life.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Niemann And Mike Corder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court Reinstates X Amid Controversy Over Speech Moderation
item.description: Justice Alexandre de Moraes lifts suspension on social platform X in Brazil, ending months-long dispute over moderation policies and misinformation
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Permitting council invests $15M in ‘surge capacity’ for agencies to manage infrastructure boom
item.description: Infrastructure projects supported by the Federal Permitting Council have seen a nearly 18-month reduction in the permitting process. The post Permitting council invests $15M in ‘surge capacity’ for agencies to manage infrastructure boom first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Lender seizes big San Jose office building in grim sign of market woes
item.description: Commercial real estate's slumping values are poised to become a widening challenge for Bay Area city, county, and regional agencies.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Databricks says with its new Databricks Apps platform, you can build tailored enterprise apps in 5 minutes
item.description: Every enterprise today is looking to get as much as possible out of its data and AI investments. But when building in-house apps, developers can struggle with infrastructure constraints and maintenance, security, data governance, compliance, and other issues.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Many loathe public speakerphone calls, so why do people do it?
item.description: The fact that it's a phone conversation — not just in-person chit-chat — could be part of the reason people find loud speakerphone calls so grating, speculated Ozlem Ayduk.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: A new tool to help startups get a foot in the door at the Pentagon
item.description: "There are only 75 PEO offices inside the four services. And I decided to go figure out who they were," said Steve Blank. The post A new tool to help startups get a foot in the door at the Pentagon first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: 5 ways data scientists can prepare now for genAI transformation
item.description: Until recently, data scientists and analysts’ primary deliverables were data visualizations, machine learning models, dashboards, reports, and analytical insights used for storytelling.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Advocacy groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending Swiss criminal probe of woman's death
item.description: Advocacy groups behind a so-called suicide capsule said Sunday they have suspended the process of taking applications to use it — which numbered over 370 last month — as a criminal investigation into its first use in Switzerland is completed.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Google's search engine's latest AI injection will answer voiced questions about video and photos
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is injecting its search engine with more artificial intelligence that will enable people to voice questions about images and occasionally organize an entire page of results, despite the technology's past misadventures with misleading information.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation
